problem_statement: Personal Assistant approval decision evidence can prove an operator approved a proposed send, but the next stage needs a separate no-effect preparation gate so approved decisions cannot be mistaken for draft creation, external send, connector mutation, memory write, or system-of-record authority.
success_criteria: A schema-backed Personal Assistant send-preparation receipt validates blocked, failed, and passed states.

Passed send-preparation receipts require valid approved approval-decision evidence, deferred decision receipt semantics, requested queue precondition binding, request queue receipt identity, approval review packet identity, and redacted preparation evidence.

The producer records send preparation evidence only and keeps external send authorization, send execution, draft creation, mailbox writes, connector mutation, memory writes, and system-of-record writes false.
The validator rejects denied or unready decisions, missing preparation refs, malformed hashes, raw recipient/message fields, secret-like values, effect drift, and missing receipt files.
The protocol manifest registers the send-preparation receipt schema.
non_goals: Do not create a Gmail draft.
Do not call Gmail, Calendar, task, messaging, browser, memory, or payment connectors.
Do not grant live connector, external-send, memory, system-of-record, deployment, customer-readiness, or production-readiness authority.
Do not serialize raw private connector payloads, recipients, subjects, message bodies, secrets, or credentials.
constraints: Approved decisions authorize only redacted send-preparation evidence, not external send.
Send execution requires a later separate receipt and final effect preflight.
